one_line_description: Free team chat app (by CAKE.com) with channels, threads, and calls, positioned as a Slack alternative.
auth_summary: API access is via an API-keys addon: after installing the addon in a workspace, users generate personal API keys with the /api-keys generate command and pass them to the HTTP API.
access_notes: Free self-serve: sign up for Pumble, install the API addon from the addon marketplace, then generate API keys in-app. No OAuth app registration flow documented for the public API.
api_surface_summary: Swagger-documented HTTP API for sending messages, replies, reactions, and listing/managing channels and users; rate limited to ~1000 requests/min per user. There is also a pumble-sdk for building bot-style apps.
main_blocker: Narrow addon-based API with per-user API keys and no OAuth app model
agent_notes: API is delivered as a marketplace addon rather than a first-class platform API; docs are thin. Only community MCP listings found (e.g. shoutkol pumble-mcp-server on LobeHub); no official Pumble MCP. Auth is API-key only per evidence found.
